name: Athena RPC: takeSnapshot
description: Add the takeSnapshot openpilot athenad RPC method. Asks the device to capture a still image from its cameras and return it base64-encoded. Reference: openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py lines 560-577 (../../openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py). The return shape is either a single base64 string or an object like {jpegBack, jpegFront} depending on device -- handle both. This unlocks the snapshot-ui feature.
acceptance_criteria: internal/ws/methods.go defines a SnapshotResult struct with optional JpegBack, JpegFront, and RawString fields so either response shape can be returned
CallTakeSnapshot(caller, client) (*SnapshotResult, error) is added; it parses both the string-only response and the {jpegBack, jpegFront} object response into SnapshotResult
The RPC uses CallWithTimeout with at least a 15-second timeout (snapshot can be slow on cold cameras)
RegisterDefaultHandlers installs a device-side stub that returns a tiny hard-coded base64 string in both fields so round-trip tests work
internal/ws/methods_test.go covers: string-shaped response, object-shaped response, and RPC-error response
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
